The corresponding smallest eigenvector captures the dominant mode of an intrinsically degree-corrected diffusion process, naturally reweighting nodes to prevent hub dominance. By transposing the data matrix and applying NBSE in feature space, we obtain a one-dimensional spectral embedding that reveals groups of redundant or semantically related dimensions; balanced binning then selects one representative per group. We prove that coloured Gaussian perturbations shift $β_N$ by at most $O(\barσ^2)$, guaranteeing robustness to measurement noise. Experiments on ImageNet embeddings from MobileNetV2 and EfficientNet-B4 show that NBSE preserves classification accuracy even under aggressive compression: on EfficientNet-B4 the accuracy drop is below $1\%$ when retaining only $30\%$ of features, outperforming ANOVA $F$-test and random selection by up to $6.8\%$.

AI.tldr: 提出基于噪声的谱嵌入(NBSE)框架，利用物理学的Nishimori温度和Bethe Hessian矩阵的奇异性，无贪心搜索地从高维数据中选择信息特征，在ImageNet嵌入上仅用30%特征即可保持分类精度下降低于1%。
AI.motivation: 现有特征选择方法多依赖贪心搜索或启发式策略，缺乏理论基础，且在高维场景下计算开销大、容易过拟合。需要一种无需贪心搜索、具有物理可解释性的鲁棒特征选择方法。
AI.method: 构建稀疏相似图，求解临界逆温度Nishimori温度β_N（Bethe Hessian奇异点），取最小特征向量作为主模式嵌入；转置数据矩阵后在特征空间应用NBSE，得到一维谱嵌入以发现冗余/语义相关特征组，再通过平衡分箱选取代表特征。
AI.result: 在MobileNetV2和EfficientNet-B4的ImageNet嵌入上，NBSE保留30%特征时精度下降<1%；在EfficientNet-B4上优于ANOVA F检验和随机选择最多6.8%。理论证明有色高斯噪声对β_N的扰动仅为O(σ̅²)，保证鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: NBSE是一种高效、鲁棒且无需贪心搜索的物理启发式特征选择方法，在极端压缩下仍能保持高分类精度，理论保证了其对测量噪声的稳定性。

title: Scalable Hyperparameter-Divergent Ensemble Training with Automatic Learning Rate Exploration for Large Models
summary:
Training large neural networks with data-parallel stochastic gradient descent allocates N GPU replicas to compute effectively identical updates -- a practice that leaves the rich space of learning rate configurations entirely unexplored during training. We propose Hyperparameter-Divergent Ensemble Training (HDET), a method that repurposes these replicas for simultaneous learning rate exploration at negligible communication overhead. HDET operates in alternating phases: a fan-out stage in which replicas train independently under a structured, symmetric spread of learning rates, and a converge stage in which parameters are averaged across all replicas via AllReduce every T steps. Building on this ensemble substrate, we further propose an automatic learning rate (auto-LR) controller that treats the relative training loss across replicas as a performance signal, updating the shared base schedule toward higher-performing configurations via a momentum-based gradient-free meta-update. The combined method produces a self-adapting learning rate schedule that improves both optimization quality and generalization without additional hyperparameter sweeps or training budget.
  Crucially, the framework generalizes beyond learning rate: any scalar hyperparameter that does not alter model architecture -- such as dropout rate, attention scale temperature, or weight-decay coefficient -- can be explored across replicas using the same fan-out/converge protocol, with inter-replica loss differences serving as zero-order hypergradients that guide the search direction. HDET is implemented as a drop-in replacement for PyTorch's OneCycleLR scheduler, requiring no changes to model architecture, optimizer, or data pipeline.

AI.tldr: 提出HDET方法，利用数据并行训练中的多个GPU副本进行超参数探索，通过扇出-收敛两阶段协议和自动学习率控制器，在不增加通信开销和训练预算的情况下实现自适应学习率调度，并可推广到其他标量超参数。
AI.motivation: 传统数据并行SGD中N个GPU副本计算几乎相同的梯度更新，浪费了探索学习率配置空间的机会。
AI.method: 两阶段协议：(1) 扇出阶段——副本在结构化的对称学习率分布下独立训练；(2) 收敛阶段——每T步通过AllReduce平均参数。同时提出自动LR控制器，利用副本间相对训练损失作为性能信号，通过基于动量的无梯度元更新来优化共享基础学习率调度。
AI.result: 该方法在无额外超参数搜索和训练预算的条件下，提升了优化质量和泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: HDET可推广到任何不改变模型结构的标量超参数（如dropout率、注意力温度、权重衰减系数），并可作为PyTorch OneCycleLR调度器的即插即用替代方案。

title: SpecRLBench: A Benchmark for Generalization in Specification-Guided Reinforcement Learning
summary: Specification-guided reinforcement learning (RL) provides a principled framework for encoding complex, temporally extended tasks using formal specifications such as linear temporal logic (LTL). While recent methods have shown promising results, their ability to generalize across unseen specifications and diverse environments remains insufficiently understood. In this work, we introduce SpecRLBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the generalization capabilities of LTL-based specification-guided RL methods. The benchmark spans multiple difficulty levels across navigation and manipulation domains, incorporating both static and dynamic environments, diverse robot dynamics, and varied observation modalities. Through extensive empirical evaluation, we characterize the strengths and limitations of existing approaches and reveal the challenges that emerge as specification and environment complexity increase. SpecRLBench provides a structured platform for systematic comparison and supports the development of more generalizable specification-guided RL methods. Code is available at https://github.com/BU-DEPEND-Lab/SpecRLBench.

AI.tldr: SpecRLBench是一个用于评估基于LTL的规范引导强化学习方法泛化能力的基准测试平台，涵盖导航和操作领域的多个难度级别，涉及静态/动态环境、多种机器人动力学和观测模态。
AI.motivation: 现有规范引导RL方法在跨未见规范和多样化环境的泛化能力方面研究不足，缺乏系统性的评估基准。
AI.method: 构建SpecRLBench基准，包含导航和操作领域的多层级难度任务，结合静态与动态环境、不同机器人动力学和观测模态，并通过大量实证评估现有方法。
AI.result: 揭示了现有方法的优势与局限，指出了随着规范和环境复杂度增加时出现的挑战。
AI.conclusion: SpecRLBench为规范引导RL方法提供了结构化比较平台，有助于推动更具泛化能力的方法开发。

title: Conflict-Aware Harmonized Rotational Gradient for Multiscale Kinetic Regimes
summary:
In this paper, we propose a harmonized rotational gradient method, termed HRGrad, for simultaneously tackling multiscale time-dependent kinetic problems with varying small parameters.
  These parameters exhibit asymptotic transitions from microscopic to macroscopic physics, making it a challenging multi-task problem to solve over all ranges simultaneously.
  Solving tasks in different asymptotic regions often encounter gradient conflicts, which can lead to the failure of multi-task learning.
  To address this challenge, we explicitly encode a hidden representation of these parameters, ensuring that the corresponding solving tasks are serialized for simultaneous training.
  Furthermore, to mitigate gradient conflicts, we segment the prediction results to construct task losses and introduce a novel gradient alignment metric to ensure a positive dot product between the final update and each loss-specific gradient.
  This metric maintains consistent optimization rates for all task losses and dynamically adjusts gradient magnitudes based on conflict levels.
  Moreover, we provide a mathematical proof demonstrating the convergence of the HRGrad method, which is evaluated across a range of challenging asymptotic-preserving neural networks (APNNs) scenarios.
  We conduct an extensive set of experiments encompassing the Bhatnagar-Gross-Krook (BGK) equation and the linear transport equation in all ranges of Knudsen number.
  Our results indicate that HRGrad effectively overcomes the `failure modes' of APNNs in these problems.

AI.tldr: 提出HRGrad方法，通过编码隐表示和梯度对齐度量，同时解决多尺度含时动力学问题中的梯度冲突，在BGK方程和线性输运方程中有效克服了APNNs的失败模式。
AI.motivation: 多尺度含时动力学问题中不同渐近区域的任务存在梯度冲突，导致多任务学习失败，现有方法难以同时求解所有尺度的动力学问题。
AI.method: HRGrad方法：1) 显式编码参数隐表示，序列化解耦不同任务；2) 分段预测结果构建任务损失；3) 引入梯度对齐度量，确保最终更新与每个损失梯度点积为正，动态调整梯度幅度。
AI.result: 在BGK方程和线性输运方程的全Knudsen数范围内，HRGrad有效克服了APNNs的"失败模式"。
AI.conclusion: HRGrad方法能有效解决多尺度动力学问题中的梯度冲突，为渐近保持神经网络提供了一种可靠的训练方案。
